data.name: AboutBadgesDescription
data.value: Every merged PR earns achievement badges based on milestones. Unlock badges for your first PR, tenth PR, earnings milestones, and top leaderboard positions. Compete on the public leaderboard ranked by total earnings. All pull requests are public.

data.name: AboutCommunityDescription
data.value: PrTask powers a thriving global community of developers and clients collaborating across borders and time zones to build better software together.

data.name: AboutContactDescription
data.value: For support or business inquiries, reach us at support@prtask.com.

data.name: AboutGetStartedDescription
data.value: Whether you are a developer looking for your next challenge or a client seeking top talent, PrTask connects you with the right people through the power of pull requests. Browse open tasks and start earning today.

data.name: AboutMissionDescription
data.value: PrTask exists to make software development accessible, fair, and rewarding for everyone. We believe that talented developers everywhere deserve equal access to meaningful work and transparent compensation regardless of where they live.

data.name: AboutOpenSourceDescription
data.value: PrTask is built on and for the open-source ecosystem. We contribute back to the community, sponsor open-source maintainers, and ensure that every line of code submitted through our platform remains accessible. Our review process is public, our leaderboard is public, and our commitment to openness is unwavering.

data.name: AboutPageDescription
data.value: PrTask is a platform where developers earn money by submitting pull requests to real projects.

data.name: AboutPaymentsDescription
data.value: Client bounty payments are processed via supported payment providers. When a client approves a pull request, they are automatically charged and the developer gets paid. No upfront deposit required. Developers choose their preferred payout method.

data.name: AboutStoryDescription
data.value: PrTask was founded with a simple idea: what if every code contribution had a clear, guaranteed bounty attached to it? Born from the frustration of unpaid open-source labor and opaque freelancing platforms, PrTask launched to create a marketplace where pull requests are the universal currency of work. From our first bounty to thousands of completed tasks, we have grown into a global community of developers and clients who share a commitment to transparent, merit-based collaboration.

data.name: AboutValueFairnessDescription
data.value: Compensation is tied directly to the work delivered. Code is reviewed on its merits, not on who wrote it. Every developer competes on a level playing field with equal opportunity.

data.name: AboutValueOpenSourceDescription
data.value: We champion open-source principles. All pull requests on PrTask are public, fostering knowledge sharing and community-driven quality standards across every project.

data.name: AboutValueTransparencyDescription
data.value: Every task, bounty, and payment is visible. Developers see exactly what they will earn before they start, and clients see exactly what they pay for. No hidden fees, no surprises.

data.name: AboutWhatWeDoDescription
data.value: Clients post development tasks with bounties. Developers claim tasks, write code, and submit pull requests. Once a PR is reviewed, approved, and merged, the developer gets paid based on the complexity of their work.

data.name: BoardEmptyStateText
data.value: No boards yet. Create one to organize your work.

data.name: BoardSeoDescription
data.value: Manage your open source project boards and track pull request tasks. Developers earn money by completing bounty tasks — a perfect side hustle for skilled programmers contributing to open source projects.

data.name: CookieConsentMessage
data.value: We use cookies to improve your experience. By using PrTask, you agree to our Cookie Policy.

data.name: CreateTaskPageDescription
data.value: Post a bounty task to attract developers.

data.name: DashboardNoPrsText
data.value: No pull requests have been submitted yet.

data.name: DownloadPageDescription
data.value: Get the PrTask app for your device.

data.name: ErrorBoundaryMessage
data.value: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

data.name: FooterDoNotSellText
data.value: Do Not Sell My Personal Information

data.name: HomeGetPaidDescription
data.value: When the client approves your PR, they are automatically charged and you get paid via your preferred payout method.

data.name: HomeHeroHeading
data.value: PrTask — Earn Money with Pull Requests

data.name: HomeHeroSubtitle
data.value: Browse tasks, submit PRs, get paid based on complexity. Climb the leaderboard.

data.name: HomePickTaskDescription
data.value: Browse open tasks with bounties ranging from simple fixes to expert-level features.

data.name: HomeSubmitPrDescription
data.value: Submit a public pull request. Multiple developers can submit PRs and the client chooses the best one.

data.name: HowItWorksStep1Description
data.value: Clients post development tasks with bounties. Browse by complexity, language, and bounty amount.

data.name: HowItWorksStep2Description
data.value: Write code and submit a public pull request. Multiple developers can submit PRs for the same task.

data.name: HowItWorksStep3Description
data.value: PRs are automatically scored based on files changed, lines added, language difficulty, and test coverage.

data.name: HowItWorksStep4Description
data.value: The client reviews all submitted PRs and approves the best one. The client is automatically invoiced for the bounty amount upon approval.

data.name: HowItWorksStep5Description
data.value: Developers receive payment via their preferred method. Earn achievement badges for each approved PR and climb the earnings leaderboard.

data.name: LeaderboardEmptyStateText
data.value: No entries yet. Be the first to submit a PR or post a task.

data.name: LoginClientsDescription
data.value: Post bounty tasks and receive high-quality code.

data.name: LoginDevelopersDescription
data.value: Browse tasks, claim work, submit PRs, earn bounties.

data.name: LoginPageDescription
data.value: Connect your account to start earning or posting tasks.

data.name: PageNotFoundMessage
data.value: The page you requested was not found.

data.name: PaymentPageDescription
data.value: View all your payments and their status.

data.name: PricingPageDescription
data.value: PrTask is completely free for everyone. No hidden fees, commissions, or charges.

data.name: SettingsKycDescription
data.value: Complete identity verification to enable payments and comply with anti-money laundering regulations.

data.name: SettingsPageDescription
data.value: Manage your account settings and payment preferences.

data.name: SprintEmptyStateText
data.value: No sprints yet. Create one to get started.

data.name: SprintSeoDescription
data.value: Organize your open source contributions into sprints. Track pull request progress, earn bounties, and manage your developer workflow. A great side hustle for developers who want to earn money through quality code.

data.name: TaskListEmptyStateText
data.value: No open tasks right now. Check back later.

data.name: TaskListPageDescription
data.value: Browse available tasks, claim them, and earn bounties.

data.name: AccessibilityAdaComplianceDescription
data.value: PrTask is committed to compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA) and equivalent international accessibility standards. We regularly audit our platform to ensure that all users, including those with disabilities, can access and use our services effectively. Our goal is to meet or exceed WCAG 2.1 Level AA requirements across all pages and features.

data.name: AccessibilityConformanceDescription
data.value: PrTask targets conformance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA, which is the standard referenced by the European Accessibility Act, the US ADA (Title III), and Section 508. We regularly audit our platform against these guidelines and remediate any identified gaps. Our conformance status is partially conformant, meaning we conform to most but not all WCAG 2.2 Level AA success criteria, and we are actively working to achieve full conformance.

data.name: AccessibilityFeedbackDescription
data.value: We welcome your feedback on the accessibility of PrTask. If you encounter any accessibility barriers, have suggestions for improvement, or need assistance accessing any part of our platform, please contact us at accessibility@prtask.com. We aim to respond to accessibility feedback within 5 business days and will work to resolve reported issues promptly. You may also request content in alternative formats. For complaints regarding accessibility that are not resolved to your satisfaction, you may contact the relevant national enforcement body in your jurisdiction.

data.name: AccessibilityKnownLimitationsDescription
data.value: While we strive to ensure accessibility across our platform, some third-party content or newly added features may not yet fully meet all WCAG 2.1 Level AA criteria. We are actively working to identify and resolve any accessibility gaps.

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureColorContrast
data.value: Sufficient color contrast ratios between text and background

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureDarkMode
data.value: Dark mode and light mode support respecting OS preferences

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureKeyboard
data.value: Keyboard navigable interface for all interactive elements

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureResponsive
data.value: Responsive design that works across devices and screen sizes

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureSemantic
data.value: Semantic HTML markup for proper screen reader navigation

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureTextAlternatives
data.value: Text alternatives for non-text content where applicable

data.name: AccessibilityOurCommitmentDescription
data.value: PrTask is committed to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities. We are continually improving the user experience for everyone and applying the relevant accessibility standards.

data.name: AcceptableUseAccountIntegrityDescription
data.value: Users must maintain one account. Creating multiple accounts to claim tasks or manipulate the system results in permanent suspension.

data.name: AcceptableUseCodeQualityDescription
data.value: All submitted pull requests must be original work. Automated or AI-generated code must be disclosed in the PR description.

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementDescription
data.value: Violations may result in account suspension, bounty forfeiture, and permanent ban from the platform.

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedDescription
data.value: Users must not use PrTask to submit plagiarized code, spam tasks, manipulate leaderboard rankings, or engage in fraudulent payment activities.

data.name: CookiePolicyAnalyticsDescription
data.value: We may use analytics cookies to understand platform usage and improve the experience. No personal data is shared with third parties for advertising.

data.name: CookiePolicyEssentialDescription
data.value: PrTask uses essential cookies to maintain your session and authentication state. These are required for the platform to function.

data.name: CookiePolicyManagingDescription
data.value: You can disable non-essential cookies through your browser settings. Disabling essential cookies may prevent you from using the platform.

data.name: CookiePolicyPaymentDescription
data.value: Payment providers may set cookies during the payment process. These are governed by the respective provider's cookie and privacy policies.

data.name: DashboardPageDescription
data.value: Track your open source pull requests, earned bounties, and developer achievements. Submit PRs to earn money — browse tasks, claim work, and get paid when your code is approved.

data.name: DmcaCounterNotificationDescription
data.value: If you believe your content was removed in error, you may file a counter-notification containing:

data.name: DmcaCounterNotificationItem2
data.value: Identification of the removed material and its prior location

data.name: DmcaCounterNotificationItem3
data.value: A statement under penalty of perjury that the material was removed by mistake or misidentification

data.name: DmcaCounterNotificationItem4
data.value: Your name, address, telephone number, and consent to jurisdiction of the federal court in your district

data.name: DmcaDesignatedAgentDescription
data.value: DMCA notices and counter-notifications should be sent to our designated agent: PrTask DMCA Agent, Email: dmca@prtask.com. Our designated agent information is registered with the U.S. Copyright Office as required by 17 U.S.C. 512(c)(2). You may also reach our designated agent by mail at the address listed on our Contact page.

data.name: DmcaFilingDescription
data.value: If you believe that content on PrTask infringes your copyright, please send a written notification to our designated agent with the following information:

data.name: DmcaFilingItem1
data.value: A physical or electronic signature of the copyright owner or authorized agent

data.name: DmcaFilingItem2
data.value: Identification of the copyrighted work claimed to have been infringed

data.name: DmcaFilingItem3
data.value: Identification of the material that is claimed to be infringing, with sufficient detail to locate it on PrTask

data.name: DmcaFilingItem4
data.value: Your contact information including address, telephone number, and email address

data.name: DmcaFilingItem5
data.value: A statement that you have a good faith belief that use of the material is not authorized by the copyright owner, its agent, or the law

data.name: DmcaFilingItem6
data.value: A statement, under penalty of perjury, that the information in the notification is accurate and that you are authorized to act on behalf of the copyright owner

data.name: DmcaOverviewDescription
data.value: PrTask respects the intellectual property rights of others and expects its users to do the same. In accordance with the Digital Millennium Copyright Act of 1998 (DMCA), we will respond promptly to claims of copyright infringement committed using our platform.

data.name: DmcaRepeatInfringersDescription
data.value: PrTask maintains a repeat infringer policy as required for DMCA safe harbor protection. Users who receive two or more valid DMCA takedown notices will have their accounts permanently terminated. PrTask reserves the right to terminate accounts of repeat infringers at its sole discretion, even on a first offense in egregious cases. Terminated users forfeit any pending bounties and are permanently barred from creating new accounts.

data.name: DmcaTakedownDescription
data.value: Upon receipt of a valid DMCA takedown notice, PrTask will expeditiously remove or disable access to the allegedly infringing material, notify the user who posted the content, and provide that user with a copy of the notice and information about how to file a counter-notification. PrTask will act promptly upon receiving a valid notice and will preserve evidence related to the allegedly infringing material for the duration of any dispute.

data.name: DownloadAndroidDescription

data.name: DownloadInstallIosDescription
data.value: Download from the App Store and tap Install. The app will appear on your home screen.

data.name: DownloadInstallLinuxDescription
data.value: Download the package for your distribution and install using your package manager.

data.name: DownloadInstallMacDescription
data.value: Download the DMG file, drag PrTask to your Applications folder, and launch from Launchpad.

data.name: DownloadInstallWindowsDescription
data.value: Download the installer, run the setup wizard, and follow the prompts to complete installation.

data.name: DownloadMacDescription

data.value: The Android APK will not install. What should I do?

data.name: DownloadTroubleshootCrash

data.name: DownloadTroubleshootCrashQuestion
data.value: The app crashes on startup. How can I fix this?

data.name: DownloadVerificationDescription
data.value: All PrTask downloads are digitally signed and verified. Check the SHA-256 checksum after downloading to ensure file integrity.

data.name: HowItWorksClientsStep1
data.value: Sign in with GitHub or Google and set up your payment method

data.name: HowItWorksClientsStep2
data.value: Create a task with a description, repository link, and bounty amount

data.name: HowItWorksClientsStep3
data.value: Developers submit public pull requests for your task

data.name: HowItWorksClientsStep4
data.value: Review all submitted pull requests and approve the best one

data.name: HowItWorksClientsStep5
data.value: You are automatically charged the bounty and the developer gets paid

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowDescription
data.value: Follow these steps to complete the full workflow from login to payment.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep1Description
data.value: Authenticate with Google, GitHub, Microsoft, or Okta.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep2Description
data.value: Complete identity verification to enable payments and comply with regulations.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep3Description
data.value: Post a task with a bounty for developers to complete.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep4Description
data.value: Upload the source code folder to the task so developers can see the diff.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep5Description
data.value: Developers browse available tasks and find work that matches their skills.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep6Description
data.value: View the task description, bounty amount, and existing pull requests.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep7Description
data.value: Edit the source code directly in the browser diff editor.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep8Description
data.value: Submit your code changes as a pull request for the task.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep9Description
data.value: Client reviews the diff to see all code changes submitted by the developer.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep10Description
data.value: Add inline comments on specific lines or highlighted code that need changes.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep11Description
data.value: Developer applies fixes, replies to comments, and resolves them.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep12Heading
data.value: Approve PR and Pay via Maya

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep12Description
data.value: Client approves the PR and pays the bounty via Maya credit card checkout.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep13Description
data.value: View all payment transactions and their status.

data.name: HowItWorksWorkflowStep14Description
data.value: Configure payout method and verify withdrawal balance.

data.name: LicensesContactDescription
data.value: For licensing inquiries, compliance questions, or credit card processing concerns, contact compliance at prtask.com.

data.name: LicensesCreditCardDescription
data.value: Credit card payments on PrTask are processed by licensed, PCI DSS-compliant payment providers. PrTask does not store, process, or transmit credit card numbers, CVVs, or other sensitive cardholder data on its own servers. All credit card transactions are handled entirely by our payment processor.

data.name: LicensesDataProtectionDescription
data.value: PrTask uses HTTPS/TLS encryption for all communications. Authentication is handled via OAuth 2.0 and OpenID Connect (OIDC) with PKCE. No plaintext credentials are stored. See our Privacy Policy for full data handling details.

data.name: LicensesDeveloperCodeDescription
data.value: Pull requests submitted by developers remain the intellectual property of their authors until a bounty is paid. Upon payment, the submitted code is licensed to the client under the license specified in the task's repository. PrTask does not claim ownership of any code submitted by developers.

data.name: LicensesOpenSourceDescription
data.value: PrTask is built with .NET, Blazor WebAssembly, and other open source technologies. These components are used under their respective licenses (MIT, Apache 2.0, etc.). Full attribution is available in the project dependency files.

data.name: LicensesPaymentProviderDescription
data.value: Our payment providers maintain current PCI DSS Level 1 certification, the highest level of security compliance in the payment card industry. They are licensed to process Visa, Mastercard, and other major card networks as applicable.

data.name: LicensesPlatformDescription
data.value: PrTask is a proprietary platform operated by PrTask. All rights to the platform, its design, branding, and source code are reserved. Use of the platform is subject to the Terms of Service.

data.name: LicensesRegulatoryDescription
data.value: PrTask complies with applicable laws and regulations regarding online payments, data protection, and consumer rights. For questions about our compliance posture, contact compliance at prtask.com.

data.name: LicensesThirdPartyDescription
data.value: PrTask incorporates the following third-party libraries and services. We gratefully acknowledge the contributions of these open-source projects and their maintainers: Microsoft .NET Runtime (MIT License), ASP.NET Core and Blazor (MIT License), Iced x86 Disassembler (MIT License), and various NuGet packages listed in our project files. For a complete list of dependencies and their licenses, see the project dependency manifest.

data.name: LoginBrowseTasksDescription
data.value: Explore available tasks filtered by language, framework, or bounty amount.

data.name: LoginGetPaidDescription
data.value: Once your PR is reviewed and merged, the bounty is released to your account automatically.

data.name: LoginHelpDescription
data.value: Visit our documentation for guides, FAQs, and support resources.

data.name: LoginNoPlatformFeesDescription
data.value: Developers keep 100% of the bounty. PrTask charges clients, never contributors.

data.name: LoginPublicPrsDescription
data.value: All work ships as real pull requests on GitHub. Your contributions are visible and verifiable.

data.name: LoginSecurityDescription
data.value: PrTask uses OAuth-based SSO exclusively. We never store passwords. All payments are held in escrow until work is verified.

data.name: LoginSignInDescription
data.value: Authenticate with your preferred SSO provider to create your PrTask account.

data.name: LoginSubmitPrsDescription
data.value: Claim a task, write the code, and open a pull request against the target repository.

data.name: LoginWhyPrTaskDescription
data.value: PrTask connects developers with paid open-source and private tasks. Ship code, earn money, and build your portfolio.

data.name: LoginEarnMoneyDescription
data.value: Get paid for every merged pull request. Bounties are funded upfront and released on merge.

data.name: PricingClientFeature1
data.value: Post unlimited tasks with custom bounties

data.name: PricingClientFeature4
data.value: Pay via credit card or supported payment method

data.name: PricingClientFeature5
data.value: Review and approve PRs before payout

data.name: PricingDevFeature1
data.value: Browse and claim tasks at no cost

data.name: PricingDevFeature2
data.value: Receive 100% of the bounty on each completed PR

data.name: PricingDevFeature4
data.value: Track your earnings and payout history

data.name: PricingDevFeature5
data.value: Get paid via your preferred payout method

data.name: PricingEnterpriseDescription
data.value: Need a tailored solution for your organization? We offer custom integrations, dedicated support, and volume-based arrangements for enterprise teams.

data.name: PricingFaqCurrencyAnswer
data.value: All transactions are in Philippine Pesos (PHP).

data.name: PricingFaqMethodsAnswer
data.value: We accept Visa, Mastercard, American Express, and other major credit and debit cards through our secure payment processor.

data.name: PricingFaqMinBountyAnswer
data.value: Yes, the minimum bounty that a client can set for a task is ₱1. There is no maximum limit.

data.name: PricingFaqMinBountyQuestion
data.value: Is there a minimum bounty amount?

data.name: PricingFaqPayoutTimeAnswer
data.value: Payouts are typically processed within 3 to 5 business days after the client approves your pull request.

data.name: PricingFaqPayoutTimeQuestion
data.value: How long until I receive my payout?

data.name: PricingFaqProcessAnswer
data.value: When a client approves a pull request, they are automatically charged the bounty amount via their payment method. The developer receives 100% of the bounty.

data.name: PricingFaqWhenPaidAnswer
data.value: Payment is processed automatically when your PR is approved by the client. Developers receive 100% of the bounty via their preferred payout method.

data.name: PricingGetStartedDescription
data.value: Join PrTask today and start earning or posting bounties. It is completely free.

data.name: PricingPaymentMethodsDescription
data.value: We accept major credit cards, debit cards, and other supported payment providers. All payments are securely processed with industry-standard encryption.

data.name: PricingPayoutDescription
data.value: Developers can withdraw earnings via bank transfer, PayPal, or other supported payout methods. Payouts are typically processed within 3 to 5 business days after PR approval.

data.name: PricingRefundDescription
data.value: We offer fair refund terms for disputed or undelivered work. Review our full refund policy for complete details.

data.name: PrivacyChildrensDescription
data.value: PrTask is not intended for users under 18 years of age. We do not knowingly collect personal data from minors. If you believe a minor has created an account, contact us for removal.

data.name: PrivacyCcpaDescription
data.value: If you are a California resident, the California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA), as amended by the California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA), grants you additional rights regarding your personal information. PrTask does not sell or share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. PrTask provides the mandatory eleven disclosures required by the CCPA in this policy, including a 12-month lookback on data categories collected. Under the CCPA/CPRA, you have the right to:

data.name: PrivacyContactDescription
data.value: For privacy inquiries, data subject requests, or to exercise any of your rights under GDPR, CCPA, RA 10173, LGPD, or any other applicable data protection law, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. For general privacy inquiries, contact privacy@prtask.com. PrTask will respond to all data subject requests within the timeframe required by applicable law (30 days for GDPR, 45 days for CCPA, 15 days for LGPD, 30 days for PIPEDA/Singapore PDPA). You also have the right to lodge a complaint with the supervisory authority in your jurisdiction.

data.name: PrivacyDataBreachDescription
data.value: PrTask maintains a comprehensive data breach response plan. In the event of a personal data breach: (a) GDPR: PrTask will notify the relevant supervisory authority within 72 hours and affected individuals when the breach is likely to result in a high risk to their rights and freedoms; (b) Philippines RA 10173: PrTask will notify the National Privacy Commission (NPC) and affected individuals within 72 hours; (c) New Zealand: notification to the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals within 72 hours for breaches likely to cause serious harm; (d) CCPA: notification to affected California residents within the timeframe required by law; (e) All jurisdictions: PrTask will document the breach, conduct a forensic investigation, implement containment measures, notify affected users with a description of the breach, the types of data involved, and recommended protective measures, and conduct a post-incident review. Our Data Protection Officer serves as the primary breach response coordinator. Contact dpo@prtask.com for data breach concerns.

data.name: PrivacyDataRetentionDescription
data.value: Account data is retained while your account is active. After account deletion, we retain: transaction records for 5 years as required by applicable tax regulations and AML record-keeping requirements; breach records for 24 months as required by PIPEDA; KYC verification records for 5 years as required by anti-money laundering laws. Anonymized and aggregated usage statistics may be retained indefinitely. We will delete or anonymize your personal data when it is no longer needed for the purpose for which it was collected, unless retention is required by law. You may request deletion of your data at any time, subject to these legal retention requirements.

data.name: PrivacyDataRightsDescription
data.value: You have the right to access your personal data held by PrTask, correct inaccurate or incomplete data, request deletion of your data (subject to legal retention requirements), and object to processing of your data.

data.name: PrivacyDataSecurityDescription
data.value: We use HTTPS encryption for all communications, GitHub OAuth tokens with minimal required scopes, and industry-standard security practices. However, no system is perfectly secure. Report vulnerabilities to security at prtask.com.

data.name: PrivacyGdprDescription
data.value: If you are located in the European Economic Area (EEA), your personal data is protected under the General Data Protection Regulation (EU) 2016/679 (GDPR). PrTask processes your data under the lawful bases of contract performance, legitimate interest, consent, and legal obligation as detailed in Section 4 above. PrTask implements privacy by design and by default (Article 25), maintains Records of Processing Activities (Article 30), and conducts Data Protection Impact Assessments for high-risk processing. PrTask recognizes and responds to Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals. As an EU data subject, you have the right to:

data.name: PrivacyInfoCollectedDescription
data.value: PrTask collects the following categories of personal data: (a) Account information: when you sign in with a supported SSO provider (GitHub, Google, Microsoft, Okta), we collect your username, display name, email address, and avatar URL; (b) Payment data: when you initiate or receive a payment, our payment provider processes payment details directly; developers provide their preferred payout method details; (c) Platform activity: task and pull request activity, code reviews, comments, and leaderboard participation; (d) Technical data: IP address, browser type, operating system, device information, and approximate geolocation derived from IP address; (e) KYC verification data: government-issued identification and selfie verification processed by our identity verification provider; (f) Tax information: tax identification numbers (W-9/W-8BEN) collected for tax compliance purposes.

data.name: PrivacyInfoSharingDescription
data.value: We share your information with the following categories of recipients: (a) Payment processors (including Maya Business and Stripe) for payment processing and developer payouts; (b) Identity verification providers for KYC compliance; (c) GitHub for authentication and repository integration; (d) Cloudflare for hosting, CDN, and security services; (e) Tax authorities when required by law (IRS, BIR, EU tax authorities under DAC7). Your public profile (username, badges, completed tasks) is visible on the leaderboard. We do not sell your personal data to third parties. We do not share your data with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. We may disclose information if required by applicable law, a valid court order, or to protect the safety and rights of PrTask and its users.

data.name: PrivacyInfoUsageDescription
data.value: We use your information to operate the PrTask platform, process bounty payments, display public leaderboard profiles and achievement badges, communicate with you about task status and payments, and prevent fraud or abuse.

data.name: PrivacyInternationalDescription
data.value: PrTask servers are hosted via Cloudflare and your data may be processed in multiple jurisdictions. For transfers of personal data from the EEA, UK, or other jurisdictions with data transfer restrictions, PrTask implements appropriate safeguards including: Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs) approved by the European Commission; UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum where applicable; adequacy decisions where the destination country has been deemed to provide adequate protection; and supplementary measures where required by transfer impact assessments. PrTask does not transfer data to countries without adequate safeguards in place. You may request a copy of the safeguards used for international transfers by contacting dpo@prtask.com.

data.name: PrivacyPaymentDataDescription
data.value: All client payment card data is handled exclusively by our payment provider. PrTask never stores credit card numbers or CVVs on our servers. Developer payout details are stored securely and shared only with the chosen payout provider. Transaction records (amount, status, date) are retained for accounting purposes.

data.name: RefundAbandonedDescription
data.value: If a task receives no pull request submissions, the client may close it at any time. Since no payment is made until PR approval, there is nothing to refund for abandoned tasks.

data.name: RefundAfterApprovalDescription
data.value: Once a client approves a pull request and is charged, the bounty is paid to the developer. Refunds are only issued in cases of platform error, fraudulent activity, or if the submitted work is materially different from what was described in the PR.

data.name: RefundAutoPaymentDescription
data.value: PrTask uses an automatic payment model. Clients are only charged when they approve a pull request. No upfront payment or deposit is required when posting a task.

data.name: RefundChargebackDescription
data.value: If a client initiates a chargeback through their bank or credit card issuer instead of using our dispute resolution process, PrTask reserves the right to suspend the client's account pending investigation. Clients who file chargebacks without first attempting resolution through PrTask may have their accounts permanently terminated. We encourage all clients to use our built-in dispute and refund process before contacting their financial institution, as chargebacks incur fees and delays for all parties involved. PrTask reserves the right to pursue recovery of funds through appropriate legal channels.

data.name: RefundDisputeDescription
data.value: If a client believes an approved PR does not meet the task requirements, PrTask initiates an arbitration process. Both parties must submit evidence within 7 business days. Possible outcomes include full refund to client if work does not meet requirements, full payment to developer if work meets the stated task requirements, or partial refund if work partially meets requirements (split determined by PrTask).

data.name: RefundHowToDescription
data.value: To request a refund, log in to your PrTask account, navigate to the task in question, and select "Request Refund." Alternatively, email support at prtask.com with your task ID and reason for the refund request. All refund requests are reviewed within 2 business days.

data.name: RefundMethodDescription
data.value: Refunds are processed to the original payment method used. Processing times depend on your bank or card issuer but typically take 5-7 business days after approval.

data.name: RefundNoChargeDescription
data.value: Clients are not charged until they explicitly approve a pull request. If no PR is approved, no payment is made. Clients may cancel a task at any time before approving a PR at no cost.

data.name: RefundNoEscrowDescription
data.value: PrTask does not hold funds in escrow. Bounties are charged directly to the client at the moment of PR approval and paid to the developer. There is no intermediary holding period. This ensures developers receive payment promptly and clients are only charged for approved work.

data.name: RefundPlatformErrorDescription
data.value: If a payment is processed incorrectly due to a platform error, PrTask will issue a full refund within 3 business days. Contact support at prtask.com with your transaction reference number.

data.name: RepositoryEmptyState
data.value: No repositories added yet. Add a repository to start posting tasks.

data.name: RepositoryPageDescription
data.value: Manage your repositories and control code visibility for tasks.

data.name: RepositoryVisibilityPrivate
data.value: Private - Only assigned developers can view the code

data.name: RepositoryVisibilityPublic
data.value: Public - Anyone can view the source code

data.name: SettingsDepositDescription
data.value: Configure payment methods for posting task bounties.

data.name: SettingsDisbursementDescription
data.value: Configure how disbursements are processed for completed tasks.

data.name: SettingsGatewaysDescription
data.value: Enable or disable payment methods for your account.

data.name: SettingsInvoiceDescription
data.value: Configure invoice generation for your transactions.

data.name: SettingsNoSavedCards
data.value: No saved cards. Add a card below.

data.name: SettingsPaymentHistoryDescription
data.value: View past transactions and payment records.

data.name: SettingsPrivacyProfilePrivate
data.value: Private - Only you can view your profile

data.name: SettingsPrivacyProfilePublic
data.value: Public - Anyone can view your profile

data.name: SettingsSavedCardsDescription
data.value: Manage your saved credit and debit cards for quick payments.

data.name: SettingsSubscriptionDescription
data.value: Manage your active subscriptions and recurring payments.

data.name: SettingsWalletDescription
data.value: Link your digital wallet for quick payments and payouts.

data.name: SettingsWithdrawalDescription
data.value: Configure how you receive payouts for completed tasks.

data.name: TermsAccountTerminationDescription
data.value: PrTask reserves the right to suspend or terminate accounts that violate these Terms, engage in fraudulent activity, or abuse the platform. Upon termination, your right to use the platform ceases immediately. Any pending bounties may be forfeited. PrTask will retain transaction records as required by applicable tax and AML regulations. You may request deletion of your personal data subject to legal retention requirements by contacting privacy@prtask.com. EU users retain the right to cancel their contract as provided by the Consumer Rights Directive.

data.name: TermsAcceptedPaymentDescription
data.value: Clients pay bounties via supported payment methods including credit cards. Developers receive payouts through their chosen method. Developers must configure their preferred payout method in their account settings before submitting pull requests.

data.name: TermsAutoPaymentDescription
data.value: Clients are automatically charged the bounty amount when they approve a pull request. By approving a PR, the client authorizes the charge. No upfront deposit is required.

data.name: TermsConfidentialityDescription
data.value: Users agree to keep confidential any non-public information obtained through the platform, including but not limited to private repository code, client business requirements, and payment details of other users.

data.name: TermsDisputeDescription
data.value: All disputes arising out of or relating to these Terms or your use of PrTask shall be resolved through the following process: (1) Users must first submit a written complaint to support@prtask.com describing the issue and desired resolution; (2) PrTask will acknowledge the complaint within 3 business days and investigate within 14 business days; (3) If the complaint is not resolved to the user's satisfaction, the dispute shall proceed to mediation administered by a mutually agreed mediator; (4) If mediation fails, the dispute shall be resolved through binding arbitration as described in the Arbitration section below. Users may also file complaints with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) or other applicable consumer protection agencies in their jurisdiction.

data.name: TermsForceDescription
data.value: PrTask shall not be liable for any failure or delay in performing its obligations where such failure or delay results from events beyond its reasonable control, including but not limited to natural disasters, war, terrorism, epidemics, internet outages, or government actions.

data.name: TermsGoverningLawDescription
data.value: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of the Philippines, without regard to conflict of law principles. Any legal action not subject to arbitration shall be brought exclusively in the courts of the Republic of the Philippines. Notwithstanding the foregoing, mandatory consumer protection laws of your jurisdiction of residence may apply to the extent they cannot be excluded by contract. EU residents retain all rights under applicable EU consumer protection regulations regardless of governing law selection.

data.name: TermsIndemnificationDescription
data.value: You agree to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless PrTask, its officers, directors, employees, and agents from and against any claims, liabilities, damages, losses, and expenses (including reasonable legal fees) arising out of or related to your use of the platform, your violation of these Terms, or your infringement of any third-party rights. This indemnification obligation does not apply to the extent prohibited by applicable mandatory consumer protection laws in your jurisdiction.

data.name: TermsIntellectualPropertyDescription
data.value: All code submitted through PrTask remains the intellectual property of its author until a bounty is paid. Upon payment, the submitted code is licensed to the client under the repository's license. PrTask retains no ownership of user-submitted code.

data.name: TermsLiabilityDescription
data.value: To the maximum extent permitted by applicable law, PrTask and its officers, employees, and affiliates shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, data, or goodwill, arising from your use of the platform. PrTask's total aggregate liability for any claims shall not exceed the total fees paid by or to you through the platform in the twelve months preceding the claim. This limitation does not apply to liability that cannot be excluded or limited under applicable law, including mandatory consumer protection laws in the EU, Philippines, and other jurisdictions.

data.name: TermsModificationDescription
data.value: PrTask reserves the right to modify these Terms at any time. Material changes will be communicated via email or a prominent notice on the platform at least 30 days before taking effect. Your continued use of PrTask after the effective date constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms. If you do not agree to the changes, you must discontinue use and close your account. Previous versions of these Terms are available upon request.

data.name: TermsPaymentDescription
data.value: When a client approves a pull request, they are automatically charged the bounty amount via their registered payment method. The full bounty is then paid to the developer. All transactions are in Philippine Pesos (PHP). There are no platform fees.

data.name: TermsPlatformFeeDescription
data.value: PrTask is a free platform. No fees are charged to clients or developers. Developers receive 100% of the bounty amount upon PR approval.

data.name: TermsPlatformRoleDescription
data.value: PrTask acts as an intermediary connecting clients who post tasks with developers who submit pull requests. PrTask does not employ developers and is not responsible for the quality of work delivered.

data.name: TermsPrivacyDescription
data.value: Your use of PrTask is subject to our Privacy Policy. By using the platform, you consent to the collection and use of your information as described in the Privacy Policy.

data.name: TermsPublicPrDescription
data.value: All pull requests submitted on PrTask are public. Multiple developers may submit pull requests for the same task. The client reviews all submissions and chooses which pull request to approve. Only the approved PR results in payment.

data.name: TermsSeverabilityDescription
data.value: If any provision of these terms is found to be unenforceable, the remaining provisions will continue in full force and effect. The unenforceable provision will be modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it enforceable.

data.name: TermsTaxDescription
data.value: Users are solely responsible for determining and fulfilling their own tax obligations arising from transactions on PrTask, including income tax reporting to the Bureau of Internal Revenue (BIR) or equivalent authority in their jurisdiction. PrTask does not withhold taxes on behalf of users and does not provide tax advice. PrTask may collect tax identification information (W-9/W-8BEN for US persons, equivalent for other jurisdictions) and issue IRS Form 1099-K for US payees exceeding applicable thresholds. PrTask complies with DAC7 reporting for EU activities and Philippine VAT requirements under RA 12023. PrTask may generate transaction summaries to assist users with tax reporting, but users must independently verify their tax obligations.

data.name: TermsUserConductDescription
data.value: Users agree to use PrTask in a lawful manner. Prohibited activities include submitting plagiarized code, manipulating leaderboard rankings, creating multiple accounts, and engaging in fraudulent payment activities. See our Acceptable Use Policy for full details.

data.name: TermsWaiverDescription
data.value: The failure of PrTask to enforce any right or provision of these terms shall not constitute a waiver of such right or provision. Any waiver must be in writing and signed by an authorized representative of PrTask.

data.name: CreateTaskVisibilityPrivate
data.value: Private - Code changes visible only to participants

data.name: CreateTaskVisibilityPublic
data.value: Public - Code changes visible to everyone

data.name: CreateTaskPaymentHint
data.value: No platform fees — developers receive 100% of the bounty.

data.name: CreateTaskSubmitAndPayButton
data.value: Create Task and Proceed to Payment

data.name: TermsIntroduction
data.value: Welcome to PrTask. These Terms of Service govern your access to and use of the PrTask platform, a developer marketplace where clients post tasks with bounties and developers submit pull requests for payment. By creating an account or using PrTask, you agree to be bound by these Terms. If you do not agree, do not use the platform.

data.name: TermsEligibilityDescription
data.value: You must be at least 18 years old and legally capable of entering into binding contracts in your jurisdiction to use PrTask. By creating an account, you represent and warrant that you meet these eligibility requirements. PrTask is not intended for users under 18 years of age in any jurisdiction. Where applicable law sets a higher age of majority, you must meet that threshold.

data.name: TermsAccountCreationDescription
data.value: To use PrTask, you must create an account by authenticating through a supported single sign-on (SSO) provider such as GitHub, Google, Microsoft, or Okta. You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials and for all activities that occur under your account. You must provide accurate and complete information during registration and keep your account information up to date. You may not create more than one account or share your account with others.

data.name: TermsDeveloperObligationsDescription
data.value: As a developer on PrTask, you agree to: submit only original work that you have the right to license; disclose the use of AI-generated code in your pull request descriptions; comply with the open source license requirements of each task's repository; complete KYC identity verification before receiving any payouts; not submit plagiarized, stolen, or unlicensed code; maintain professional conduct in all platform interactions; and comply with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction, including tax reporting obligations.

data.name: TermsClientObligationsDescription
data.value: As a client on PrTask, you agree to: provide clear and accurate task descriptions and requirements; set fair and appropriate bounty amounts for the work requested; review submitted pull requests in a timely and good-faith manner; authorize payment upon approving a pull request; maintain a valid payment method on your account; not post fraudulent, misleading, or illegal tasks; and comply with all applicable laws and regulations in your jurisdiction.

data.name: TermsRefundRightsDescription
data.value: Clients may request a refund if a pull request was approved in error or if the delivered work materially fails to meet the task requirements. Refund requests must be submitted within 7 days of payment. PrTask will review refund requests within 5 business days and process eligible refunds through the original payment method. For EU residents, the 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services applies as detailed in our Refund Policy. For Philippine residents, consumer protection rights under RA 7394 are fully honored. See our full Refund Policy for complete details.

data.name: TermsLicenseGrantsDescription
data.value: Upon approval and payment of a bounty, the developer grants the client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to use, modify, reproduce, and distribute the submitted code, subject to any open source license requirements of the repository. The developer retains copyright ownership of their code. PrTask is granted a non-exclusive, worldwide license to display, cache, and reproduce submitted code solely for the purpose of operating and improving the platform. Developers represent and warrant that they have the legal right to grant these licenses and that their submissions are free of third-party intellectual property claims.

data.name: TermsArbitrationDescription
data.value: All disputes arising out of or relating to these Terms or your use of PrTask that are not resolved through the internal dispute resolution process shall be resolved through binding arbitration administered by a mutually agreed arbitral institution. You agree to waive the right to a jury trial and to participate in a class action. You may opt out of this arbitration agreement within 30 days of account creation by sending written notice to legal@prtask.com. Small claims court actions are exempt from this arbitration requirement. EU residents retain the right to access Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) mechanisms and may file complaints with the applicable consumer arbitration board in their Member State.

data.name: TermsDisclaimerDescription
data.value: The PrTask platform is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis without warranties of any kind, whether express or implied. PrTask disclaims all warranties, including implied warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, title, and non-infringement. PrTask does not guarantee the quality, accuracy, or completeness of any work submitted by developers, nor does it guarantee that the platform will be uninterrupted, secure, or error-free. This disclaimer does not affect mandatory consumer rights that cannot be excluded under applicable law, including EU consumer protection regulations.

data.name: TermsConsumerProtectionDescription
data.value: PrTask complies with Republic Act No. 7394 (Consumer Act of the Philippines) and equivalent consumer protection laws in all jurisdictions where the platform operates. Users are entitled to fair and equitable treatment, accurate information about services and pricing, and access to effective complaint and redress mechanisms. PrTask maintains transparent pricing with no hidden fees, provides clear service descriptions for all tasks and bounties, and ensures that users can exercise their consumer rights without obstruction. EU users benefit from the protections of the Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU), including pre-contractual information requirements and the right to cancel distance contracts.

data.name: TermsAmlComplianceDescription
data.value: PrTask complies with Republic Act No. 9160 (Anti-Money Laundering Act) as amended by RA 11521, EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives (AMLD6), and applicable AML laws in all jurisdictions. All users must complete Know Your Customer (KYC) verification before receiving payouts. PrTask monitors transactions for suspicious activity and reports covered and suspicious transactions to the Anti-Money Laundering Council (AMLC) and relevant authorities as required by law. Users must not use PrTask to launder money, finance terrorism, or facilitate any illegal financial activity. PrTask reserves the right to freeze accounts and withhold funds pending investigation of suspicious activity.

data.name: TermsEuConsumerRightsDescription
data.value: EU residents have a 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services under the Consumer Rights Directive. This right may be waived at the point of purchase with your explicit consent and acknowledgment that you lose the withdrawal right once the digital service is fully performed. PrTask provides clear pre-contractual information about prices, main characteristics of services, and trader identity. From June 2026, PrTask will provide a clear "Cancel my contract" function as required by EU law. PrTask participates in Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) as required by the Digital Services Act. Users may contact the applicable consumer arbitration board in their EU Member State for out-of-court dispute settlement.

data.name: TermsContentModerationDescription
data.value: In compliance with the EU Digital Services Act (DSA) 2022/2065, PrTask maintains transparent content moderation policies. Users may flag illegal or harmful content through our reporting mechanism. PrTask provides a statement of reasons for every content moderation decision and offers an internal complaint handling mechanism for users affected by moderation decisions. Users have access to out-of-court dispute settlement. PrTask publishes annual transparency reports on content moderation activities. PrTask prohibits dark patterns in all user interfaces and ensures that content moderation decisions are made in a non-discriminatory manner with due regard for fundamental rights.

data.name: TermsEntireAgreementDescription
data.value: These Terms, together with the Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, Acceptable Use Policy, Refund Policy, DMCA Policy, and any other policies referenced herein, constitute the entire agreement between you and PrTask regarding your use of the platform. These Terms supersede all prior agreements, understandings, and communications between you and PrTask, whether oral or written.

data.name: TermsContactDescription
data.value: For questions about these Terms of Service, contact us at legal@prtask.com. For privacy inquiries, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. For accessibility concerns, contact accessibility@prtask.com. For abuse reports, contact abuse@prtask.com.

data.name: PrivacyIntroduction
data.value: PrTask is committed to protecting your privacy and personal data. This Privacy Policy explains how PrTask collects, uses, shares, and protects your personal information when you use our platform. This policy applies to all users worldwide and addresses the specific requirements of data protection laws in the European Union, United Kingdom, California (USA), Philippines, Canada, Brazil, Japan, Singapore, Thailand, South Africa, Australia, India, South Korea, China, and New Zealand.

data.name: PrivacyControllerDescription
data.value: PrTask is the data controller responsible for your personal data. Our Data Protection Officer (DPO) can be reached at dpo@prtask.com. For general privacy inquiries, contact privacy@prtask.com. Our registered address is available on our Contact page. If you are located in the EU and PrTask does not have an EU establishment, we will appoint an EU representative as required by Article 27 of the GDPR.

data.name: PrivacyCcpaCategoriesDescription
data.value: Under the California Consumer Privacy Act, the categories of personal information we have collected in the past 12 months include: identifiers (name, email address, username, IP address); commercial information (transaction history, bounty payments); internet or electronic network activity (browsing history on PrTask, feature usage, log data); professional information (GitHub profile, portfolio, skills); and geolocation data (approximate location derived from IP address). We do not collect sensitive personal information as defined by the CCPA. We do not sell or share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising purposes.

data.name: PrivacyLegalBasisDescription
data.value: We process your personal data under the following lawful bases: (a) Contract performance: processing necessary to fulfill our contract with you, including account management, payment processing, and platform operation; (b) Legitimate interest: processing necessary for our legitimate interests, including fraud prevention, platform security, and service improvement, where these interests are not overridden by your rights; (c) Consent: where you have given specific consent for processing, such as marketing communications and non-essential cookies; (d) Legal obligation: processing necessary to comply with applicable laws, including tax reporting, AML compliance, and data breach notification requirements. You may withdraw consent at any time without affecting the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal.

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightAccess
data.value: Access a copy of all personal data we hold about you

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightRectify
data.value: Rectify inaccurate or incomplete personal data

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightErase
data.value: Erase your personal data (right to be forgotten), subject to legal retention obligations

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightRestrict
data.value: Restrict processing of your personal data in certain circumstances

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightPortability
data.value: Data portability: receive your data in a structured, machine-readable format

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightObject
data.value: Object to processing based on legitimate interests, including profiling

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightWithdrawConsent
data.value: Withdraw consent at any time where processing is based on consent

data.name: PrivacyGdprRightLodgeComplaint
data.value: Lodge a complaint with your local supervisory authority

data.name: PrivacyGdprContactInfo
data.value: To exercise any of these rights, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com. We will respond within 30 days as required by the GDPR. PrTask recognizes and responds to the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal as a valid opt-out request.

data.name: PrivacyUkGdprHeading
data.value: UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018

data.name: PrivacyUkGdprDescription
data.value: If you are located in the United Kingdom, your personal data is protected under the UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018, enforced by the Information Commissioner's Office (ICO). You have the same rights as EU data subjects listed above, adapted to UK law. PrTask maintains a lawful basis for processing UK residents' data and implements appropriate technical and organizational security measures. If PrTask does not have a UK establishment, we will appoint a UK representative as required. To exercise your rights or file a complaint, contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com or the ICO at ico.org.uk.

data.name: PrivacyCcpaRightKnow
data.value: Know what personal information we collect, use, and disclose about you, including a 12-month lookback

data.name: PrivacyCcpaRightDeletion
data.value: Request deletion of your personal information, subject to legal exceptions

data.name: PrivacyCcpaRightOptOut
data.value: Opt out of the sale or sharing of your personal information (PrTask does not sell personal information)

data.name: PrivacyCcpaRightNonDiscrimination
data.value: Non-discrimination for exercising your CCPA/CPRA rights

data.name: PrivacyCcpaRightLimit
data.value: Limit the use of sensitive personal information (PrTask does not collect sensitive personal information as defined by CCPA)

data.name: PrivacyCcpaContactInfo
data.value: To submit a CCPA/CPRA request, email privacy@prtask.com with the subject line "CCPA Request." We will verify your identity before processing the request and respond within 45 days. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out of the sale or sharing of personal information.

data.name: PrivacyRa10173Description
data.value: If you are a Philippine citizen or resident, your personal data is protected under Republic Act No. 10173 (Data Privacy Act of 2012), enforced by the National Privacy Commission (NPC). PrTask complies with the principles of transparency, legitimate purpose, and proportionality. You have the right to: be informed of the purpose and extent of processing; access your personal data; object to processing; erasure or blocking of personal data; rectify inaccurate data; file a complaint with the NPC; and claim damages for unauthorized processing. PrTask has appointed a Data Protection Officer and will register with the NPC as required for entities processing sensitive personal information. Contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyLgpdDescription
data.value: If you are located in Brazil, your personal data is protected under the Lei Geral de Protecao de Dados (LGPD), enforced by the ANPD. PrTask processes your data under lawful bases including consent, contract performance, and legitimate interest. You have the right to: confirmation of data processing; access your data; correct incomplete or inaccurate data; anonymize, block, or delete unnecessary data; data portability; information about sharing with third parties; information about consent withdrawal; and revoke consent at any time. PrTask will respond to data subject requests within 15 days. Contact our DPO at dpo@prtask.com to exercise your LGPD rights.

data.name: PrivacyPipedaDescription
data.value: If you are a Canadian resident, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act (PIPEDA), enforced by the Office of the Privacy Commissioner of Canada (OPC). PrTask adheres to the ten Fair Information Principles: accountability, identifying purposes, consent, limiting collection, limiting use and disclosure, accuracy, safeguards, openness, individual access, and challenging compliance. You have the right to access your personal information and challenge its accuracy. PrTask will respond to access requests within 30 days. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint.

data.name: PrivacyAppiDescription
data.value: If you are located in Japan, your personal information is protected under the Act on the Protection of Personal Information (APPI), enforced by the Personal Information Protection Commission (PPC). PrTask publishes the purpose of data use, implements cybersecurity and physical safeguards, and provides an opt-out mechanism for third-party data sharing. We will notify the PPC and affected individuals in the event of a data breach. You have the right to request disclosure, correction, cessation of use, and deletion of your personal information. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyPdpaSingaporeDescription
data.value: If you are located in Singapore, your personal data is protected under the Personal Data Protection Act 2012 (PDPA), enforced by the Personal Data Protection Commission (PDPC). PrTask has appointed a Data Protection Officer whose contact information is publicly available. You have the right to access and correct your personal data within 30 days. PrTask will notify the PDPC of any data breach that is likely to result in significant harm or is of a significant scale. We retain your personal data only as long as necessary for the purpose for which it was collected. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyPdpaThailandDescription
data.value: If you are located in Thailand, your personal data is protected under the Personal Data Protection Act B.E. 2562 (PDPA), enforced by the Personal Data Protection Committee (PDPC). PrTask obtains consent before collecting, using, or disclosing your personal data. You have the right to: access your data; obtain a copy in a readable format; object to processing; request erasure or destruction; request suspension of use; and withdraw consent. PrTask implements appropriate security safeguards and conducts regular audits. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyPopiaDescription
data.value: If you are located in South Africa, your personal information is protected under the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA), enforced by the Information Regulator. PrTask complies with the eight conditions for lawful processing: accountability, processing limitation, purpose specification, further processing limitation, information quality, openness, security safeguards, and data subject participation. You have the right to access, correct, and delete your personal information. PrTask will notify the Information Regulator and affected individuals of any security compromise as soon as reasonably possible. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyAustraliaDescription
data.value: If you are located in Australia, your personal information is protected under the Privacy Act 1988 and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs), enforced by the Office of the Australian Information Commissioner (OAIC). PrTask maintains an up-to-date privacy policy and participates in the Notifiable Data Breaches scheme. You have the right to access and correct your personal information. PrTask does not use automated decision-making that significantly affects you without disclosure. PrTask complies with AI disclosure requirements effective December 2026. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint with the OAIC.

data.name: PrivacyDpdpDescription
data.value: If you are located in India, your digital personal data is protected under the Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 (DPDP), enforced by the Data Protection Board of India. PrTask obtains your consent through clear affirmative action before processing your data. You have the right to: access a summary of your personal data and processing activities; correct and update your data; erase your data; nominate another person to exercise your rights; and file a grievance. PrTask maintains a grievance redressal mechanism and will respond to your requests promptly. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a grievance.

data.name: PrivacyPipaDescription
data.value: If you are located in South Korea, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA), enforced by the Personal Information Protection Commission (PIPC). PrTask obtains truly voluntary consent (not bundled or coercive) for data processing. You have the right to: access your data; correct inaccurate data; request suspension of processing; request deletion; and data portability in a secure machine-readable format (effective March 2025). PrTask provides transparency on algorithmic processes and user profiling. If PrTask does not have a domestic establishment, we will appoint a domestic representative as required. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyPiplDescription
data.value: If you are located in China, your personal information is protected under the Personal Information Protection Law (PIPL), enforced by the Cyberspace Administration of China (CAC). PrTask does not bundle consent for multiple processing purposes and obtains separate consent for sensitive personal information. You have the right to: know about and decide on processing; restrict or refuse processing; access and copy your data; data portability; correct inaccurate data; request deletion; and request an explanation of processing rules. PrTask conducts Personal Information Protection Impact Assessments as required. Contact dpo@prtask.com to exercise your rights.

data.name: PrivacyNzDescription
data.value: If you are located in New Zealand, your personal information is protected under the Privacy Act 2020, enforced by the Office of the Privacy Commissioner. PrTask complies with the 13 Information Privacy Principles. You have the right to access and correct your personal information. PrTask will notify the Privacy Commissioner and affected individuals within 72 hours of any breach that causes or is likely to cause serious harm. PrTask complies with cross-border disclosure restrictions. Contact privacy@prtask.com to exercise your rights or file a complaint with the Privacy Commissioner.

data.name: PrivacyCookieReferenceDescription
data.value: PrTask uses cookies and similar technologies as described in our Cookie Policy. Essential cookies are required for platform operation. Analytics and marketing cookies require your opt-in consent in the EU, UK, and other jurisdictions that mandate prior consent. You can manage your cookie preferences at any time. See our Cookie Policy for full details including a complete inventory of cookies used, their purposes, and retention periods.

data.name: PrivacyAutomatedDecisionDescription
data.value: PrTask does not currently use automated decision-making or profiling that produces legal effects or similarly significant effects on users. If PrTask introduces such processing in the future, we will provide meaningful information about the logic involved, the significance, and the envisaged consequences, and will ensure that you have the right to obtain human intervention, express your point of view, and contest the decision, as required by GDPR Article 22 and equivalent provisions in other jurisdictions.

data.name: PrivacyDoNotSellHeading
data.value: Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information

data.name: PrivacyDoNotSellDescription
data.value: PrTask does not sell your personal information to third parties. PrTask does not share your personal information with third parties for cross-context behavioral advertising. If this practice changes in the future, PrTask will provide a prominent "Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information" link and a "Limit the Use of My Sensitive Personal Information" link as required by the CCPA/CPRA. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out request.

data.name: PrivacyThirdPartyDescription
data.value: PrTask integrates with third-party services including GitHub for authentication and repository integration, payment processors (including Maya Business) for transaction handling, Cloudflare for hosting and CDN, and identity verification providers for KYC compliance. Each third-party service operates under its own privacy policy. We only share the minimum data necessary for each integration to function. We maintain Data Processing Agreements (DPAs) with all third-party processors as required by GDPR and applicable data protection laws.

data.name: PrivacyChangesDescription
data.value: PrTask may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via email or a prominent notice on the platform at least 30 days before taking effect. We review and update this policy at least annually. The "Last updated" date at the top of this policy indicates the most recent revision. Your continued use of PrTask after the effective date of changes constitutes acceptance of the updated policy. Previous versions of this policy are available upon request.

data.name: CookiePolicyIntroduction
data.value: This Cookie Policy explains how PrTask uses cookies and similar tracking technologies when you visit or use our platform. It describes what cookies are, the types we use, why we use them, and how you can control or disable them. This policy should be read together with our Privacy Policy.

data.name: CookiePolicyWhatAreDescription
data.value: Cookies are small text files stored on your device (computer, tablet, or mobile phone) when you visit a website. They help the site remember your preferences, understand how you use the site, and improve your experience. Cookies can be "persistent" (remaining on your device for a set period) or "session" cookies (deleted when you close your browser). Cookies can be set by the site you visit ("first-party cookies") or by other services that provide content on the page ("third-party cookies").

data.name: CookiePolicyFunctionalDescription
data.value: Functional cookies remember your preferences such as theme selection (dark or light mode), language settings, and notification preferences. These cookies enhance your experience but are not strictly required for the platform to operate. You may disable functional cookies without affecting core functionality, but your preferences will not be remembered between sessions.

data.name: CookiePolicyThirdPartyDescription
data.value: Certain third-party services integrated with PrTask may set their own cookies. These include our payment processor for secure transaction handling and GitHub for OAuth authentication. Third-party cookies are governed by the respective provider's cookie and privacy policies. PrTask does not control these cookies. Third-party cookies are only loaded after you have provided consent where required by applicable law.

data.name: CookiePolicyCsrfPurpose
data.value: Protects against cross-site request forgery attacks

data.name: CookiePolicyThemePurpose
data.value: Remembers your selected theme (dark or light mode)

data.name: CookiePolicyGaPurpose
data.value: Google Analytics identifier for unique visitor tracking

data.name: CookiePolicyConsentDescription
data.value: When you first visit PrTask, a cookie consent banner informs you about our use of cookies and allows you to accept all, reject all, or customize your preferences by category. Essential cookies are always active and cannot be disabled. Analytics, functional, and marketing cookies require your consent. You may update your cookie preferences at any time through your account settings or by clearing your browser cookies and revisiting the site.

data.name: CookiePolicyEuConsentDescription
data.value: Under the ePrivacy Directive and GDPR (EU) and PECR (UK), prior opt-in consent is required before setting non-essential cookies. PrTask implements an opt-in model: no non-essential cookies are placed until you explicitly consent. The reject button has equal prominence to the accept button. PrTask does not use cookie walls (denying access for refusing non-essential cookies). Consent is granular per category (analytics, functional, marketing). PrTask maintains auditable records of consent and will request renewal periodically.

data.name: CookiePolicyCcpaConsentDescription
data.value: Under the California Consumer Privacy Act, PrTask discloses all cookies used and their purposes. PrTask honors Global Privacy Control (GPC) signals as a valid opt-out of any sale or sharing of personal information through cookies. PrTask does not sell personal information collected through cookies.

data.name: CookiePolicyOtherRegionsDescription
data.value: Brazil (LGPD), Thailand (PDPA), South Korea (PIPA), India (DPDP), South Africa (POPIA), and China (PIPL) require consent for non-essential cookies. PrTask applies the opt-in consent model globally to ensure compliance with the strictest applicable requirements. Japan (APPI), Canada (PIPEDA), Singapore (PDPA), and Australia follow notification or implied consent models for non-sensitive cookies, but PrTask provides opt-in consent for all users regardless of jurisdiction.

data.name: CookiePolicyWithdrawDescription
data.value: You may withdraw your cookie consent at any time by clicking the cookie preferences link in the platform footer, adjusting settings in your account preferences, or clearing all PrTask cookies through your browser settings and revisiting the site. Withdrawing consent does not affect the lawfulness of processing based on consent before withdrawal. After withdrawal, non-essential cookies will be removed and will not be set again until you provide new consent.

data.name: CookiePolicyRetentionDescription
data.value: Essential cookies (session, CSRF) are retained for the duration of your session or up to 30 days. Cookie consent preferences are retained for 1 year. Functional cookies (theme, language) are retained for 1 year. Analytics cookies are retained for up to 2 years. Marketing cookies are retained for up to 3 months. All cookie retention periods are listed in the cookie inventory table above.

data.name: CookiePolicyPrivacyLinkDescription
data.value: This Cookie Policy is part of and should be read in conjunction with our Privacy Policy. The Privacy Policy provides comprehensive information about how PrTask collects, uses, and protects your personal data, including data collected through cookies. If there is any conflict between this Cookie Policy and the Privacy Policy, the Privacy Policy prevails.

data.name: CookiePolicyContactDescription
data.value: For questions about our use of cookies or to exercise your privacy rights related to cookie data, contact our Data Protection Officer at dpo@prtask.com or our privacy team at privacy@prtask.com.

data.name: RefundIntroduction
data.value: This Refund Policy describes when and how refunds are issued on the PrTask platform. PrTask uses an automatic payment model where clients are charged only upon approval of a pull request. This policy covers all refund scenarios, including applicable consumer protection rights under EU, Philippine, and other jurisdictions' laws.

data.name: RefundEuWithdrawalDescription
data.value: If you are an EU or EEA resident, you have a 14-day right of withdrawal for digital services under the Consumer Rights Directive (2011/83/EU). This right begins from the date of the transaction. You may exercise this right without giving any reason by contacting support@prtask.com within 14 days. PrTask will process your refund within 14 days of receiving your withdrawal notification using the same payment method as the original transaction. This withdrawal right may be waived at the point of purchase if you give explicit prior consent and acknowledge that you lose the right of withdrawal once the digital service (bounty payment to developer) is fully performed. If PrTask fails to inform you of the withdrawal right, it is automatically extended to 1 year.

data.name: RefundPhConsumerDescription
data.value: Philippine residents are entitled to consumer protection under Republic Act No. 7394 (Consumer Act of the Philippines). If a service fails to meet the description or quality standards represented at the time of purchase, you are entitled to a full refund or remediation. You may file a complaint with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) if your refund request is not resolved satisfactorily through PrTask's internal process. PrTask will cooperate fully with any DTI investigation.

data.name: RefundNonRefundableDescription
