data.value: The following are not eligible for refunds: bounties for pull requests that were reviewed, approved, and where the code has been merged and meets the task requirements; disputes filed more than 30 days after the transaction; and situations where the client has already used, deployed, or incorporated the submitted code into production. EU residents' statutory withdrawal rights are not affected by this section where those rights have not been validly waived.

data.name: RefundTimelineDescription
data.value: PrTask processes refunds within the following timelines: EU withdrawal right refunds within 14 days of receiving the withdrawal notice; platform error refunds within 3 business days; dispute resolution refunds within 5 business days of the resolution decision; and all other eligible refunds within 5 business days of approval. Actual receipt of the refund depends on your bank or payment provider and may take an additional 5-10 business days.

data.name: RefundContactDescription
data.value: For refund requests or questions about this policy, contact support@prtask.com with your task ID and transaction reference number. For EU consumers exercising the right of withdrawal, include "EU Withdrawal" in your subject line. For Philippine consumer complaints, you may also contact the DTI directly.

data.name: AcceptableUseIntroduction
data.value: This Acceptable Use Policy governs your conduct on the PrTask platform. All users, including clients and developers, must comply with this policy. Violations may result in enforcement actions including account suspension or termination. This policy complies with the EU Digital Services Act (DSA) content moderation requirements.

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedPlagiarism
data.value: Submitting plagiarized, stolen, or unlicensed code in pull requests

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedSpam
data.value: Spamming tasks, pull requests, or platform communications

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedLeaderboard
data.value: Manipulating leaderboard rankings or achievement badges through artificial means

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedFraud
data.value: Engaging in fraudulent payment activities or payment manipulation

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedPaymentCircumvent
data.value: Attempting to circumvent the automatic payment system

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedHarassment
data.value: Harassing, threatening, or intimidating other users

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedMalware
data.value: Uploading malware, viruses, backdoors, or malicious code

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedScraping
data.value: Scraping or automated data collection from the platform without authorization

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedImpersonation
data.value: Impersonating another user or misrepresenting your identity

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedMoneyLaundering
data.value: Using the platform to launder money, finance terrorism, or facilitate illegal transactions

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedLowQuality
data.value: Intentionally submitting low-quality work to collect bounties

data.name: AcceptableUseProhibitedCollusion
data.value: Colluding with other users to manipulate task outcomes or artificially inflate earnings

data.name: AcceptableUseConductDescription
data.value: The following conduct is strictly prohibited on PrTask and may result in immediate enforcement action:

data.name: AcceptableUseConductMoneyLaundering
data.value: Using the platform to facilitate money laundering, terrorism financing, or any violation of the Anti-Money Laundering Act (RA 9160)

data.name: AcceptableUseConductKycCircumvent
data.value: Circumventing or attempting to circumvent KYC verification requirements

data.name: AcceptableUseConductFakeTasks
data.value: Creating fake tasks or pull requests to artificially inflate earnings or manipulate payment flows

data.name: AcceptableUseConductExploiting
data.value: Exploiting platform vulnerabilities, APIs, or payment systems for unauthorized gain

data.name: AcceptableUseConductInauthentic
data.value: Engaging in coordinated inauthentic behavior including shill bidding, fake reviews, or rating manipulation

data.name: AcceptableUseConductAutomated
data.value: Using automated tools or bots to interact with the platform without prior written authorization

data.name: AcceptableUseConductMalicious
data.value: Distributing, hosting, or linking to malicious software, phishing pages, or exploit kits

data.name: AcceptableUseConductIpViolation
data.value: Violating the intellectual property rights of others, including submitting code that infringes copyrights or patents

data.name: AcceptableUseConductDiscrimination
data.value: Engaging in discriminatory, hateful, or threatening behavior toward any user based on race, gender, religion, nationality, disability, or any other protected characteristic

data.name: AcceptableUseConductUnauthorizedAccess
data.value: Attempting to access other users' accounts, data, or payment information without authorization

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementWarningText
data.value: A formal notice identifying the violation and requiring corrective action

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementContentText
data.value: Removal of offending tasks, pull requests, or other content

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementSuspensionText
data.value: Suspension of account access for a defined period (7 to 90 days)

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementTerminationText
data.value: Permanent removal from the platform with forfeiture of pending bounties

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementFinancialText
data.value: Freezing of account funds pending investigation of financial violations

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementLegalText
data.value: Reporting of criminal activity to law enforcement authorities, including the AMLC for suspected money laundering

data.name: AcceptableUseEnforcementAppeal
data.value: PrTask reserves the right to take enforcement action at its sole discretion. Users subject to enforcement action may appeal by contacting abuse@prtask.com within 14 days of the action. PrTask will provide a statement of reasons for every content moderation decision as required by the EU Digital Services Act.

data.name: AcceptableUseContentModerationDescription
data.value: In compliance with the EU Digital Services Act (DSA) 2022/2065, PrTask maintains transparent content moderation policies. Users may flag illegal or harmful content through our reporting mechanism. PrTask processes notices from "trusted flaggers" with priority. PrTask provides a statement of reasons for every content moderation decision, offers an internal complaint handling mechanism, and provides access to out-of-court dispute settlement for users affected by moderation decisions. PrTask publishes annual transparency reports on content moderation activities and decisions. PrTask prohibits dark patterns in content moderation interfaces and ensures that algorithmic decision-making is subject to human review.

data.name: AcceptableUseReportingDescription
data.value: If you observe any activity that violates this Acceptable Use Policy, please report it to abuse@prtask.com. Include the username of the offending party, a description of the violation, and any supporting evidence such as links or screenshots. All reports are reviewed within 2 business days. PrTask protects the identity of reporters and prohibits retaliation against anyone who submits a good-faith report.

data.name: AcceptableUseContactDescription
data.value: For questions about this Acceptable Use Policy, contact abuse@prtask.com. For legal inquiries, contact legal@prtask.com.

data.name: LicensesOpenSourceGratitude
data.value: We are grateful to the open source community for making these tools available. For a complete list of dependencies and their licenses, see the project dependency manifest.

data.name: LicensesThirdPartyGitHub
data.value: GitHub: used for OAuth authentication and pull request integration, subject to GitHub's Terms of Service

data.name: LicensesThirdPartyPayment
data.value: Payment processors (including Maya Business): used for secure transaction handling, subject to their respective service agreements

data.name: LicensesThirdPartyCloudflare
data.value: Cloudflare: used for hosting, CDN, and DDoS protection, subject to Cloudflare's Terms of Service

data.name: LicensesUserContentDescription
data.value: By posting content on PrTask (including task descriptions, comments, and reviews), you grant PrTask a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to display, reproduce, and distribute that content solely for the purpose of operating and improving the platform. You retain all ownership rights to your content. This license terminates when you delete your content from PrTask, except where the content has been shared with or relied upon by other users.

data.name: LicensesCodeOwnershipDescription
data.value: Developers retain full copyright ownership of all code they submit through PrTask. Upon approval and payment of a bounty, the developer grants the client a perpetual, worldwide, non-exclusive, irrevocable license to use, modify, reproduce, and distribute the submitted code, subject to the open source license of the repository. PrTask does not claim ownership of any code submitted by developers. Developers must warrant that they have the right to submit all code and that it does not infringe third-party intellectual property rights. AI-generated code must be disclosed in the pull request description, and the developer must warrant they have the right to license it.

data.name: AccessibilityEaaDescription
data.value: PrTask complies with the European Accessibility Act (EU) 2019/882, effective June 28, 2025, and the harmonized standard EN 301 549 V3.2.1. This requires that our website and web applications conform to WCAG 2.1 Level AA at minimum for users in the EU. PrTask is committed to meeting the updated WCAG 2.2 Level AA standard as the EN 301 549 V4.1.1 standard is adopted. We apply these accessibility standards to all users regardless of their geographic location.

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22Description
data.value: PrTask targets conformance with the Web Content Accessibility Guidelines (WCAG) 2.2 at Level AA, which includes the following new criteria beyond WCAG 2.1:

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22FocusNotObscured
data.value: 2.4.11 Focus Not Obscured (Minimum): focused elements are not entirely hidden by other content

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22FocusAppearance
data.value: 2.4.13 Focus Appearance: focused elements have a clearly visible focus indicator

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22DraggingMovements
data.value: 2.5.7 Dragging Movements: alternatives to drag-and-drop interactions are provided

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22TargetSize
data.value: 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum): interactive targets are at least 24x24 CSS pixels

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22ConsistentHelp
data.value: 3.2.6 Consistent Help: help mechanisms are in the same relative location across pages

data.name: AccessibilityWcag22RedundantEntry
data.value: 3.3.7 Redundant Entry: users are not required to re-enter information they have already provided

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureFocusIndicators
data.value: Clear focus indicators on all interactive elements for keyboard navigation

data.name: AccessibilityMeasureTargetSize
data.value: Minimum interactive target size of 24x24 CSS pixels

data.name: DmcaSafeHarborDescription
data.value: PrTask qualifies for safe harbor protection under Section 512 of the Digital Millennium Copyright Act (17 U.S.C. 512) as an online service provider. PrTask has registered a designated DMCA agent with the U.S. Copyright Office, implements and publishes a notice-and-takedown process, maintains a counter-notification procedure, has adopted and reasonably implements a repeat infringer policy, and accommodates standard technical measures used by copyright owners to identify or protect copyrighted works.

data.name: DmcaCounterProcessDescription
data.value: Upon receipt of a valid counter-notification, PrTask will forward it to the original complainant. If the complainant does not file a court action seeking a restraining order against the subscriber within 10 business days, PrTask will restore the removed content within 10 to 14 business days of receiving the counter-notification.

data.name: DmcaEuCopyrightDescription
data.value: In compliance with Article 17 of the EU Copyright Directive (Directive 2019/790), PrTask cooperates with rights holders and maintains a complaint and redress mechanism for copyright disputes. PrTask makes best efforts to obtain authorization for user-uploaded content where required and implements effective measures to prevent the availability of infringing content upon receiving notice from rights holders. Users whose content is affected by copyright enforcement actions have the right to file a complaint through our internal mechanism.

data.name: HomeDescriptionHeading
data.value: What Is PrTask — The Developer Marketplace for Pull Request Bounties

data.name: HomeDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask is a developer marketplace that connects clients who need software work done with skilled developers who complete tasks through pull requests. The platform operates on a bounty-based model where clients post tasks with monetary rewards, and developers earn those bounties by submitting high-quality code that passes review. Every task on PrTask is tied to a real GitHub repository, ensuring that all contributions are transparent, verifiable, and integrated directly into production codebases.

data.name: HomeDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Unlike traditional freelancing platforms, PrTask focuses exclusively on pull request-based work. This means developers can browse open tasks, submit their solutions as pull requests, and receive automatic payment once their code is approved and merged by the task owner. The review process follows standard GitHub workflows, making it familiar to any developer who has contributed to open source or worked in a team environment. Bounties range from small bug fixes to complex feature implementations, giving developers of all skill levels an opportunity to earn.

data.name: HomeDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: PrTask charges zero platform fees to developers — they receive 100% of every bounty earned. Clients set the bounty amount when creating a task, and payment is processed automatically upon pull request approval. The platform supports multiple payment gateways including credit cards, GCash, GrabPay, Maya, bank transfers, and QR Ph payments. Whether you are a solo developer looking for side income or a company seeking specialized talent for specific tasks, PrTask provides a streamlined workflow from task creation to payment disbursement.

data.name: AboutDescriptionHeading
data.value: About PrTask — Our Mission and Values

data.name: AboutDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask was founded on the belief that software development talent exists everywhere, but opportunity does not. Our mission is to democratize access to paid development work by creating a transparent marketplace where skill and code quality are the only factors that matter. We built PrTask to eliminate the barriers that prevent talented developers in emerging markets from accessing well-paying software work, while simultaneously helping clients find skilled developers who can deliver production-ready code through a familiar pull request workflow.

data.name: AboutDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Our core values of transparency, fairness, and open source commitment guide every decision we make. Transparency means all tasks, bounties, and reviews are publicly visible so both clients and developers can make informed decisions. Fairness means developers keep 100% of their bounty earnings with no hidden fees or deductions. Our commitment to open source means we actively support the ecosystem by enabling paid contributions to open source projects and maintaining our own platform code with community input.

data.name: AboutDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: PrTask is more than a marketplace — it is a community of developers and clients who share a vision of how software work should operate. We provide badges and XP to recognize achievements, maintain a leaderboard to highlight top contributors, and offer comprehensive payment options that work across borders. From our founding story as a project to empower Filipino developers to our growth into a global platform, PrTask remains committed to fair pay, quality code, and building trust between clients and developers through the universal language of pull requests.

data.name: TaskListDescriptionHeading
data.value: Browse Open Tasks — Find Paid Development Work

data.name: TaskListDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Tasks page is your gateway to finding paid development work on PrTask. Every task listed here represents a real piece of software work posted by a client, complete with a bounty that you earn upon successful completion. Tasks span a wide range of complexity levels from trivial fixes that take minutes to expert-level implementations requiring deep domain knowledge. You can filter tasks by complexity level to find work that matches your skill set, and sort by newest or highest bounty to prioritize your search.

data.name: TaskListDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Each task card displays essential information at a glance: the complexity rating, bounty amount in PHP, a brief description, and relevant tags indicating the technologies involved. Complexity levels include Trivial, Simple, Medium, Complex, and Expert, with bounty amounts typically scaling with difficulty. Tags help you quickly identify tasks that match your technical expertise, whether that is C#, Blazor, Python, JavaScript, DevOps, or any other technology stack. Click on any task to view the full description, requirements, and linked GitHub repository.

data.name: TaskListDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: Bounty amounts are determined by clients when they create a task and reflect the value of the work being requested. PrTask does not charge developers any fees — the bounty amount you see is the exact amount you receive upon approval. To get started, browse the available tasks, find one that matches your skills, review the requirements in the full task detail page, and submit your solution as a pull request. The entire workflow follows standard GitHub practices, making it accessible to any developer familiar with version control and code review processes.

data.name: DashboardDescriptionHeading
data.value: Your PrTask Dashboard — Platform Overview and Activity Tracking

data.name: DashboardDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Dashboard provides a centralized overview of platform activity on PrTask. At a glance, you can see the total number of active tasks available on the platform, the number of registered users, the total bounty pool across all open tasks, and the count of pull requests submitted. These statistics give you a real-time snapshot of the marketplace health and help you understand the current level of activity and opportunity available on the platform.

data.name: DashboardDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: For developers, the Dashboard serves as a starting point to assess the current landscape of available work. When active tasks are listed, they appear directly on your dashboard with quick links to view details and start working. For clients, the Dashboard provides visibility into the status of tasks you have created, including any pending pull requests awaiting your review. The recent pull requests section shows the latest submissions across the platform, keeping you informed about ongoing development activity.

data.name: DashboardDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: The Dashboard updates dynamically as new tasks are created, pull requests are submitted, and bounties are paid out. If you are a client looking to post new work, you can navigate directly to the task creation form from the Dashboard. If you are a developer looking for opportunities, the statistics help you gauge demand and competition. As the platform grows, the Dashboard will continue to serve as your command center for tracking all aspects of your PrTask activity, from earnings and submissions to task management and team collaboration.

data.name: LoginDescriptionHeading
data.value: Sign In to PrTask — Secure Authentication Options

data.name: LoginDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask uses single sign-on (SSO) authentication through trusted identity providers to ensure your account security. You can sign in using your existing GitHub, Google, Microsoft Azure, or Okta account — no need to create a separate username and password for PrTask. This approach leverages the enterprise-grade security of these providers, including their two-factor authentication, suspicious login detection, and account recovery mechanisms, so your PrTask account benefits from the same protections you already have in place.

data.name: LoginDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Authentication is required to access most features on PrTask because financial transactions and code contributions require verified identities. When you sign in for the first time, PrTask creates your account automatically and links it to your chosen identity provider. Developers can connect their GitHub accounts to streamline the pull request submission process, while clients benefit from verified identities that build trust in the marketplace. All authentication data is transmitted over encrypted connections and PrTask never stores your identity provider passwords.

data.name: LoginDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: For developers, signing in unlocks the ability to browse tasks, submit pull requests, earn bounties, and track your leaderboard ranking. For clients, authentication enables task creation, bounty funding, pull request review, and payment management. PrTask supports multiple connected accounts, so you can link your GitHub for code submissions and your Google account for general authentication. Visit the Settings page after signing in to manage your connected accounts, set up payment methods, and complete your KYC verification for bounty withdrawals.

data.name: PricingDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask operates on a fundamentally developer-friendly pricing model: developers pay nothing and keep 100% of every bounty they earn. There are no subscription fees, no commission percentages, and no hidden charges deducted from developer payouts. When a client posts a task with a ₱500 bounty, the developer who completes and gets their pull request approved receives the full ₱500. This pricing structure reflects our belief that the people writing the code should receive the full value of their work.

data.name: PricingDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: For clients, PrTask is also free to use. Clients pay only the bounty amount they set when creating tasks, plus standard payment processing fees charged by the payment gateway (credit card, bank transfer, or mobile wallet). There are no platform subscription fees, no markup on bounties, and no ongoing costs. Enterprise clients who need custom integrations, dedicated support, or volume task management can contact us for tailored solutions. Our pricing transparency extends to the feature comparison table above, which confirms that all core features are included for both developers and clients at no cost.

data.name: PricingDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: Payment processing on PrTask supports multiple methods to accommodate users worldwide. Clients can fund bounties using credit cards, bank transfers, GCash, GrabPay, Maya, and QR Ph payments. Developer payouts are processed automatically when a pull request is approved, with disbursement available via bank transfer or PayPal. The minimum bounty amount ensures meaningful compensation for development work, and all transactions are processed securely through PCI DSS compliant payment infrastructure. Review our refund policy for details on how payments are handled in case of disputes or task cancellations.

data.name: HowItWorksDescriptionHeading
data.value: How PrTask Works — Complete Guide to Earning and Hiring

data.name: HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask streamlines the process of connecting developers with paid software work through a pull request-based workflow that mirrors how professional development teams already operate. The process begins with signing up using your GitHub, Google, Microsoft, or Okta account, which takes seconds and requires no separate registration form. Once authenticated, developers can immediately browse available tasks, while clients can start creating tasks and setting bounties right away. The entire lifecycle from task creation to payment disbursement follows a predictable, transparent workflow with clear steps at every stage.

data.name: HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: For developers, the workflow is straightforward: browse tasks, find one matching your skills, review the requirements and linked GitHub repository, implement the solution in your own fork, and submit a pull request through PrTask. The task owner reviews your code following standard GitHub review practices, may request changes, and ultimately approves and merges your contribution. Upon merge, payment is triggered automatically — no invoicing, no payment requests, no waiting for manual processing. The bounty is disbursed directly to your configured payout method.

data.name: HowItWorksDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: For clients, PrTask simplifies the hiring process by eliminating interviews, contracts, and scope negotiations. Create a task with a clear description, set the complexity level and bounty amount, link it to your GitHub repository, and publish it to the marketplace. Developers worldwide can then discover and work on your task. You review submissions as pull requests with full code diff visibility, request changes if needed, and approve when satisfied. The platform handles payment processing, developer verification, and dispute resolution, letting you focus on reviewing code quality rather than managing freelancer relationships.

data.name: DownloadDescriptionHeading
data.value: Download PrTask — Available on Multiple Platforms

data.name: DownloadDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: PrTask is available as a native application on multiple platforms, giving you the flexibility to manage tasks, review pull requests, and track payments from your preferred device. The Android app provides a full-featured mobile experience with offline support, push notifications, and biometric authentication. The Windows desktop application offers a native experience with system tray integration and local file access. Linux users can download the standalone binary for x64 systems, and the web application is always accessible at prtask.com from any modern browser.

data.name: DownloadDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Each platform version of PrTask shares the same core functionality built with .NET MAUI and Blazor technology, ensuring a consistent experience across devices. Native apps offer additional capabilities like offline access to cached task data, push notifications for pull request reviews and payment updates, and biometric login for enhanced security. The web application requires no installation and works on any device with a modern browser, making it the fastest way to get started. iOS and macOS versions are currently in development and will be available in a future release.

data.name: DownloadDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: Installation is straightforward on all supported platforms. Android users can download the APK directly and enable installation from unknown sources in their device settings. Windows users download the executable and run it — no installer required. Linux users download the binary, set the executable permission, and run it from the terminal. All downloads are verified and signed to ensure authenticity. Check the feature comparison table above to understand which capabilities are available on each platform, and refer to the troubleshooting section if you encounter any issues during installation.

data.name: LeaderboardDescriptionHeading
data.value: PrTask Leaderboard — Rankings, Earnings, and Recognition

data.name: LeaderboardDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The PrTask Leaderboard showcases the top-performing developers and most active clients on the platform. Rankings are determined by objective, verifiable metrics: for developers, the leaderboard tracks the number of pull requests merged and total bounty earnings; for clients (top spenders), it tracks total bounties posted and funded. This dual-view leaderboard gives visibility to both sides of the marketplace, helping developers find active clients and helping clients identify experienced, proven developers.

data.name: LeaderboardDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Developer rankings are based on real accomplishments — every merged pull request and every dollar earned is backed by actual code contributions visible on GitHub. There are no subjective ratings, no client-assigned scores, and no pay-to-rank schemes. The leaderboard updates in real time as pull requests are approved and bounties are paid out. Top earners gain visibility that can lead to more task invitations and higher-value opportunities. PrTask also awards XP points and badges for achievements like completing your first task, earning streaks, and reaching milestone earnings.

data.name: LeaderboardDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: The leaderboard serves multiple purposes in the PrTask ecosystem. For new developers, it provides aspirational targets and demonstrates the earning potential of the platform. For clients, it acts as a talent directory where they can discover developers with proven track records of delivering quality code. For the community as a whole, it creates a healthy competitive environment that encourages excellence and continuous improvement. Switch between the Top Earners and Top Spenders tabs to explore both sides of the marketplace and find the developers or clients that best match your needs.

data.name: SprintDescriptionHeading
data.value: Sprint Management — Organize Time-Boxed Development Work

data.name: SprintDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: Sprints on PrTask bring agile project management methodology to the bounty-based development model. A sprint is a time-boxed period — typically one to four weeks — during which specific tasks are planned, executed, and delivered. Sprint management allows clients and team leads to organize related tasks into focused work periods with clear start dates, end dates, and goals. This structured approach helps teams maintain momentum, track progress against deadlines, and ensure that development work aligns with broader project milestones.

data.name: SprintDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Creating a sprint is straightforward: give it a name, set a goal that describes what should be accomplished, and define the start and end dates. Sprints progress through three statuses — Planned, Active, and Completed — giving you clear visibility into where each sprint stands. You can start planned sprints when the team is ready, complete active sprints when the time box expires, or delete sprints that are no longer needed. Each sprint tracks the tasks assigned to it, providing a focused view of what needs to be done within the time period.

data.name: SprintDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: Sprint management on PrTask integrates with the broader task and board systems to provide a complete project management experience. Assign tasks from the task list to specific sprints to create a sprint backlog, track completion rates as developers submit pull requests, and review sprint outcomes to improve planning for future iterations. Whether you are running a small open source project with a handful of contributors or managing a large-scale development effort across multiple repositories, sprint management helps you maintain predictable delivery cadences and keep your development work organized and on schedule.

data.name: BoardDescriptionHeading
data.value: Kanban Boards — Visual Task Management and Workflow

data.name: BoardDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: Boards on PrTask provide a visual, column-based approach to managing development tasks using the Kanban methodology. Each board displays tasks organized into workflow stages — typically columns like To Do, In Progress, In Review, and Done — giving you an at-a-glance view of where every task stands in the development pipeline. This visual representation makes it easy to identify bottlenecks, balance workloads, and ensure that tasks flow smoothly from creation through completion and payment.

data.name: BoardDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Creating a board is simple: provide a name and select the board type (Kanban is the default). Once created, you can add tasks to the board and organize them across columns to reflect their current status. Boards support drag-and-drop interaction for moving tasks between stages, making workflow management intuitive and fast. You can create multiple boards for different projects, repositories, or teams, keeping your work organized in ways that make sense for your specific development workflow and organizational structure.

data.name: BoardDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: PrTask boards sync in real time via WebSocket connections, so changes made by any team member are immediately visible to everyone viewing the board. This real-time collaboration is essential for distributed teams where developers may be working across different time zones. Boards integrate with the PrTask task system, so when a developer submits a pull request for a task on your board, the task status updates automatically. Combined with sprint management and the leaderboard, boards complete the project management toolkit that PrTask provides, enabling teams to plan, execute, track, and celebrate their development work in one unified platform.

data.name: SettingsDescriptionHeading
data.value: Account Settings — Configure Your PrTask Experience

data.name: SettingsDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Settings page is your central hub for configuring every aspect of your PrTask account. From here, you can update your profile information including display name, avatar, and email address. You can configure notification preferences to control which events trigger email alerts — choose to receive all notifications, only important ones like payment confirmations and review requests, or disable email notifications entirely. Your profile settings determine how other users see you on the platform, including on the leaderboard and in pull request reviews.

data.name: SettingsDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Payment configuration is a critical part of your account settings. Developers need to set up a withdrawal method (bank transfer or PayPal) to receive bounty payouts, while clients configure deposit methods (credit card or bank transfer) to fund task bounties. PrTask supports multiple payment gateways including QR Ph, credit cards, GCash, GrabPay, Maya, and direct bank transfers — each can be individually enabled or disabled based on your preference. You can also manage saved payment cards, configure automatic invoicing, and set up QR code-based payments for quick transactions.

data.name: SettingsDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: Security and verification settings protect your account and enable access to all platform features. Connect multiple identity providers (Google, GitHub, Microsoft, Okta) to your account for flexible authentication options. Enable two-factor authentication via authenticator app or email for additional security. Complete the KYC (Know Your Customer) verification process by submitting a government-issued ID to unlock higher withdrawal limits and build trust with clients. Privacy settings let you control the visibility of your profile and activity, and timezone configuration ensures dates and deadlines display correctly for your location.

data.name: CreateTaskDescriptionHeading
data.value: Create a Task — Post Bounties for Development Work

data.name: CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Create Task page is where clients post new development work to the PrTask marketplace. Each task requires a clear title, detailed description, linked GitHub repository URL, and a bounty amount that developers will earn upon successful completion. The task creation form guides you through setting all the parameters that help developers understand what needs to be done, how complex the work is, and what they will earn for completing it. Well-defined tasks attract more qualified developers and lead to faster, higher-quality submissions.

data.name: CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Complexity levels (Trivial, Simple, Medium, Complex, Expert) help developers gauge the expected effort and skill requirements before committing to a task. Set the complexity honestly to attract the right developers — underestimating complexity may result in incomplete submissions, while overestimating may deter developers from attempting the task. The bounty amount should reflect the complexity and value of the work. PrTask enforces a minimum bounty to ensure meaningful compensation, and developers see the full bounty amount with no hidden deductions. Tags help categorize your task by technology and type for better discoverability.

data.name: CreateTaskDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: You can also set work item types (Epic, Feature, Story, Task, Bug) to align with your project management methodology, and choose between public and private visibility. Public tasks are visible to all developers on the marketplace, while private tasks are only accessible to invited developers. After submitting a task, it becomes immediately available on the Tasks page where developers can discover and start working on it. Payment for the bounty is processed when you approve and merge the winning pull request, ensuring you only pay for work that meets your requirements and passes your code review.

data.name: RepositoryDescriptionHeading
data.value: Repository Management — Connect and Configure Your GitHub Repos

data.name: RepositoryDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Repository page allows clients to connect their GitHub repositories to PrTask for seamless task management and pull request workflows. By linking a repository, you enable developers to submit pull requests directly against your codebase when working on tasks you have posted. Each connected repository can have a custom display name for easy identification within PrTask, and visibility settings control whether the repository is publicly listed or restricted to specific developers.

data.name: RepositoryDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Managing your connected repositories is essential for maintaining an organized development workflow on PrTask. When you create a task, you link it to one of your connected repositories so developers know exactly where their code contributions should be submitted. Public repositories are visible to all marketplace participants, making them ideal for open source projects and community-driven development. Private repositories restrict access to invited developers only, suitable for proprietary codebases where you want to control who can view and contribute to your code.

data.name: RepositoryDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: PrTask integrates with GitHub through standard API connections, requiring only the repository URL to establish the link. You maintain full control over your repositories — PrTask does not modify repository settings, branch protections, or access permissions. All pull request reviews and merges are performed through your existing GitHub workflow, with PrTask handling the bounty payment and task status tracking automatically. As you add more repositories, the Repository page serves as your central directory for all codebases connected to the PrTask marketplace.

data.name: TermsDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: These Terms of Service constitute the complete legal agreement between you and PrTask governing the use of our platform. They cover all aspects of the developer marketplace including account creation, task posting, pull request submissions, payment processing, intellectual property rights, dispute resolution, and platform conduct requirements. The terms are designed to protect both developers and clients while maintaining a fair, transparent marketplace for bounty-based software development.

data.name: TermsDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask operates under Philippine law and complies with international regulations including GDPR for European users, CCPA for California residents, and consumer protection laws across multiple jurisdictions. These terms address platform fees (zero for developers, transparent for clients), automatic payment processing upon pull request approval, refund rights and dispute resolution procedures, content moderation policies, and KYC/AML compliance requirements. We encourage all users to read these terms thoroughly and contact legal@prtask.com with any questions.

data.name: PrivacyDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Privacy Policy explains in detail how PrTask collects, uses, stores, and protects your personal information across all our services and platforms. It covers data from account registration, task interactions, pull request submissions, payment processing, and general platform usage. PrTask is committed to data minimization — we collect only the information necessary to operate the marketplace, process payments, and comply with legal requirements.

data.name: PrivacyDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: This policy addresses compliance with privacy laws from over 20 jurisdictions worldwide, including the GDPR (European Union), UK GDPR, CCPA (California), RA 10173 (Philippines), LGPD (Brazil), PIPEDA (Canada), APPI (Japan), PDPA (Singapore and Thailand), POPIA (South Africa), and more. Each section outlines your specific rights under the applicable law and how to exercise them. PrTask never sells your personal data and provides mechanisms for data access, correction, deletion, and portability upon request.

data.name: CookiePolicyDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Cookie Policy provides complete transparency about the cookies and similar tracking technologies used on the PrTask platform. We use a minimal set of cookies necessary for platform functionality, user preferences, and anonymous analytics. Each cookie is documented in the reference table above with its name, provider, purpose, duration, and category so you can make informed decisions about your cookie preferences.

data.name: CookiePolicyDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask respects your cookie choices in accordance with EU ePrivacy Directive, GDPR, CCPA, and other regional privacy regulations. Essential cookies that are required for platform security and functionality cannot be disabled, but you have full control over functional and analytics cookies. You can manage your cookie preferences at any time through your browser settings or our cookie consent mechanism, and withdraw consent for non-essential cookies without affecting your ability to use the core features of the platform.

data.name: RefundDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Refund Policy outlines the conditions under which payments made through PrTask may be refunded. Because PrTask uses an automatic payment model where bounties are disbursed upon pull request approval, the refund process is designed to be fair to both clients and developers. The policy covers scenarios including disputed work quality, abandoned tasks, platform errors, and regulatory requirements under EU and Philippine consumer protection laws.

data.name: RefundDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask does not charge developers until a pull request is approved, and clients are not charged until they explicitly fund a task bounty. This pay-on-approval model minimizes the need for refunds by ensuring that payment only occurs when both parties agree on the delivered work. If a refund is necessary, PrTask processes it through the original payment method within the timelines specified in this policy. Contact support@prtask.com to initiate a refund request or dispute a transaction.

data.name: AcceptableUseDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Acceptable Use Policy establishes the rules of conduct for all participants in the PrTask marketplace. It defines prohibited activities including plagiarism, spam, leaderboard manipulation, payment fraud, harassment, and other behaviors that undermine the integrity of the platform. These rules exist to protect the community and ensure that PrTask remains a trustworthy environment for both developers seeking paid work and clients seeking quality code contributions.

data.name: AcceptableUseDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: Enforcement actions range from warnings to permanent account termination depending on the severity and frequency of violations. PrTask employs both automated detection and manual review to identify policy violations, and provides an appeal process for users who believe enforcement actions were applied in error. All users are expected to maintain professional conduct, submit original work, and comply with applicable laws regarding financial transactions, intellectual property, and anti-money laundering regulations.

data.name: LicensesDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Licenses page provides comprehensive information about the software licenses, payment provider agreements, regulatory compliance certifications, and intellectual property policies that govern the PrTask platform. It covers the platform license itself, credit card processing compliance, payment provider integrations, data protection certifications, and the open source components that PrTask is built upon. Understanding these licenses helps you know your rights and obligations when using the platform.

data.name: LicensesDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask is built using open source technologies including .NET, Blazor, and various community libraries, and we are committed to respecting and acknowledging the licenses of all components we use. Code submitted by developers through pull requests remains the intellectual property of the submitter unless the task specifies otherwise, and the license terms for contributed code are determined by the repository license of the client's project. This page also details third-party service integrations with GitHub, payment processors, and Cloudflare that are essential to platform operations.

data.name: AccessibilityDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This Accessibility Statement documents PrTask's commitment to making the platform usable by everyone, including people with disabilities. We target WCAG 2.2 Level AA conformance and comply with the Americans with Disabilities Act (ADA), the European Accessibility Act (EAA), and other regional accessibility requirements. Our accessibility measures include semantic HTML markup, sufficient color contrast ratios, full keyboard navigation support, responsive design, text alternatives for non-text content, dark mode, clear focus indicators, and minimum interactive target sizes.

data.name: AccessibilityDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask actively works to identify and address accessibility barriers. If you encounter any accessibility issues while using the platform, we encourage you to contact us so we can resolve them promptly. We regularly audit our platform against WCAG 2.2 criteria and prioritize fixes for any conformance gaps. Our goal is to ensure that all users — regardless of ability — can create tasks, browse opportunities, submit pull requests, manage payments, and participate fully in the PrTask developer marketplace.

data.name: DmcaDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: This DMCA Policy outlines PrTask's procedures for handling copyright infringement claims under the Digital Millennium Copyright Act (17 U.S.C. 512) and the EU Copyright Directive (Directive 2019/790). As a platform where developers submit code through pull requests, PrTask takes intellectual property protection seriously. We maintain a registered DMCA agent, implement a notice-and-takedown process, provide counter-notification procedures, and enforce a repeat infringer policy to protect both copyright holders and legitimate contributors.

data.name: DmcaDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: If you believe that code submitted through PrTask infringes on your copyright, this policy explains how to file a takedown notice with the required information. If your code was removed and you believe the takedown was in error, the counter-notification process allows you to contest the removal. PrTask processes all DMCA requests promptly and fairly, balancing the rights of copyright holders with the legitimate contributions of developers. For copyright-related inquiries, contact our designated DMCA agent using the information provided in this policy.

data.name: CreateTaskTitlePlaceholder
data.value: Fix authentication bug in user service

data.name: PaymentDescriptionParagraph1
data.value: The Payment History page provides a comprehensive view of all financial transactions associated with your PrTask account. Whether you are a developer receiving bounty payouts or a client funding task bounties, this page gives you full transparency into every payment made through the platform. Each transaction record includes the date, associated task, amount, current status, and payment type so you can track your finances with confidence.

data.name: PaymentSeoDescription
data.value: View your payment history and transaction details.

data.name: PaymentDescriptionParagraph3
data.value: For clients, this page also serves as a record of all bounty disbursements made to developers who completed your tasks. You can cross-reference payments with specific tasks to verify that funds were distributed correctly. If you have questions about a specific transaction or need to dispute a payment, PrTask provides a dedicated support channel accessible from your account settings. All payment data is encrypted and stored securely in compliance with PCI DSS standards.

data.name: PaymentDescriptionParagraph2
data.value: PrTask processes payments automatically when a pull request is approved and merged by the task owner. Developers receive 100% of the bounty amount with no platform fees deducted from their earnings. Payment statuses update in real time, allowing you to see whether a transaction is pending, completed, or requires attention. You can filter your payment history by entering your user ID to view transactions specific to your account.
